title: Follow-up questions that keep conversation alive
subtitle: React, ask, and invite the other person to continue.
duolingoBridge: B1 Med trains native-style but level-appropriate French: natural chunks, connected answers, listening strategy, and practical output.
overview: Knowledge point 1: ask for detail after an answer.
Knowledge point 2: use natural follow-up questions, not only prepared questions.

sections.title: Natural tools
rows: qu’est-ce que tu veux dire par là ?
what do you mean by that?
tu peux me donner un exemple ?
can you give me an example?
what do you think about it?

items.fr: Tu dis que c’était difficile; comment ça exactement ?
items.en: You say it was difficult; how exactly?

items.fr: Et après, qu’est-ce que tu as fait ?
items.en: And then, what did you do?

items.fr: Quand tu dis que c’était bizarre, qu’est-ce que tu veux dire par là ?
items.en: When you say it was strange, what do you mean by that?
items.es: Cuando dices que fue raro, ¿qué quieres decir con eso?

items.fr: Tu peux me donner un exemple concret ?
items.en: Can you give me a concrete example?

items.fr: Et toi, tu en penses quoi maintenant ?
items.en: And you, what do you think about it now?
items.es: ¿Y tú, qué piensas de eso ahora?
